quotes.verbatim_quote:
One of the strongest ways to make chords connect smoothly is to treat the notes of each one
SEPARATELY.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core voice-leading principle: chords as parallel lines.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
When thinking of the notes of the chords in this manner, each note is called a voice;
the way that a voice moves from chord to chord will be called voice leading.

One important chord change that may use the principle of voice leading is V7 to I. Believe it or not,
this one simple chord change is the basis for many, many sounds in music.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Asserts V7-I as the fundamental voice-leading laboratory.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The 3rd of the V7 chord moves up to the root of the I chord in each example; this is common
procedure for V7 to I.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States the leading-tone resolution rule explicitly.

quotes.verbatim_quote: leaving out the Sth in a I chord is acceptable in a case such as this.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Permits 5th omission when voice leading is strong.

quotes.topic: Voice leading paramount for modern chords
quotes.verbatim_quote: In moving modern chords, voice leading is of the utmost importance.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Elevates voice leading to a top priority in modern harmony.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Using a chord %
step above or below the next chord is acceptable if good voice leading is employed.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Permits chromatic approach chords when voice-leading-justified.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Quite often following a minor 7th type chord a dominant chord
whose root is a 4th higher will be used.

Anticipations such as these
are acceptable in most cases.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Permits rhythmic anticipation of upcoming chord.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
if you lower ONE root ina
major chord that has TWO roots, you will get a major 7th chord.

There are certain kinds of chords which almost necessitate the use of voice leading principles. One
of these is the m# TYPE
quotes.why_load_bearing: Identifies m(maj7) as a chord requiring voice leading.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
This involves
breaking down each chord into its tones, and analyzing each voice.

quotes.topic: Voice leading justifies all chord chart
quotes.verbatim_quote:
It is largely due to voice leading and systematic thinking that so many chords are listed in the
reference charts.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Justifies the reference charts: every chord earns its place via voice leading.

quotes.topic: All chords usable with voice leading
quotes.verbatim_quote:
With
good voice leading, they all can be used
quotes.why_load_bearing: Universal claim: any chord works if voice-led correctly.
